taskRelevance: First factory run for fantasy-writer domain completed. 29 meta-knowledge entries created across 2 categories: craft (15 entries covering hierarchy design, cross-cutting agents, adversarial patterns, quality tiers, convergence, artifact protocols, pipeline structure, evaluation patterns, and domain decomposition) and factory-operations (14 entries covering pipeline bottlenecks, agent performance baselines, gap convergence patterns, invariant violation patterns, and iteration patterns). All entries at confidence=low pending confirmation from future runs.

insights.summary: Mixed depth-2/depth-3 hierarchy optimal for 9+ phase creative pipelines. Use depth-2 for ≤3 specialists, depth-3 for 5+ groups.
Cross-cutting concerns (consistency, series knowledge, craft enforcement) should be orchestrator-level shared specialists, not per-phase duplicates.
Adversarial auditor-per-phase pattern with distributed invariants (81 across 8 auditors) provides full coverage without overloading agents.
Two-tier quality: non-negotiable invariants (always-on) + selectable craft toolbox (chosen per-story, then binding). Avoids over-constraining.
Multi-writer artifacts MUST use read-modify-write protocol, not create-once. Default to read-modify-write when >1 writer.
Ghost agent references appear after roster restructuring. Add automated cross-reference sweep after any agent rename/split/merge.
Guide agent needs explicit exemption from ask_questions prohibition since its role requires user input gathering.
Initial 67-agent prompt writing is the dominant bottleneck (~269min). First-pass rejection by reviewer forces full rewrite cycle.
Prompt-reviewer shows reject-once-then-approve pattern. Budget for one rewrite cycle on initial submissions.
Gap convergence follows exponential decay (~73% reduction per cycle). 4 of 10 allowed cycles needed for 67-agent system.
